pr.title: Split-up improvements across common, GM/MN, TY, and baselib
pr.author: fjooord

summary: Split-up matching PR touching 12 files across camera, common fighter bury code, GM/MN menu code, map collision, TY display, and sysdolphin/baselib. The PR body and bot report claim 5 new exact matches, 8 unmatched-item improvements, +1608 matched code bytes, +24 matched data bytes, and no reported regressions.

key_files.role: Adjusted stack/local shape in Camera_80030CFC; bot report lists it as a new exact match.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: src/melee/cm/camera.c added 2 deleted 1
raw_diff: Camera_80030CFC adds u8 _PAD[12] and changes PAD_STACK(14) to PAD_STACK(2)
text_corpus: bot report lists main/melee/cm/camera Camera_80030CFC as 99.83% to 100.00%

key_files.role: Added tightly scoped force_active sdata2 literals and removed an 8-byte dummy local from ftCo_800C0D0C; bot report lists .sdata2 and ftCo_800C0D0C as new exact matches.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: adds #pragma force_active on/reset around ftCo_804D8C28, ftCo_804D8C30, ftCo_804D8C38, ftCo_804D8C3C
raw_diff: ftCo_800C0D0C removes u8 _[8] = { 0 }
human_pr_text: notes force_active .sdata2 literals correspond to existing symbols.txt entries and ftCo_Bury.c split-owned .sdata2 range
text_corpus: bot report lists ftCo_Bury .sdata2 and ftCo_800C0D0C as new matches

key_files.role: Added a narrow PAD_STACK in fn_8018325C and changed fn_801884F8 result cache access to pointer indexing; bot report lists fn_801884F8 as a new exact match.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_8018325C adds PAD_STACK(4)
raw_diff: fn_801884F8 changes lbl_80473700.result_cache accesses to int* ptr indexing at ptr[65] and ptr[66]
human_pr_text: says pointer indexing mirrors nearby pointer-style access in fn_8018846C
text_corpus: bot report lists fn_801884F8 as 99.82% to 100.00%

key_files.role: Scoped mnCount_8025092C locals inside an added block with PAD_STACK(4), preserving the existing selection/ranking logic while improving unmatched score.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: src/melee/mn/mncount.c added 49 deleted 46
raw_diff: mnCount_8025092C wraps entries/tmp/i/j/min locals and logic inside a nested block after PAD_STACK(4)
text_corpus: bot report lists mnCount_8025092C as 99.92% to 99.94%

key_files.role: Refactored mpCheckFloor distance calculation to reuse a declared dist2 local and SQ macro expressions; added small padding local.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mpCheckFloor adds u8 pad[4] and float dist2
raw_diff: replaces dx/dy/dx2/dy2 temporary sequences with dist2 = SQ(px_sp54 - ax) + SQ(py_sp50 - ay)
text_corpus: bot report lists mpCheckFloor as 99.68% to 99.88%

key_files.role: Adjusted local declaration/order and category/index temporaries in un_8031BC54; PR notes that earlier string-base/assert reshaping for another function was dropped.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_8031BC54 moves id declaration after gobj and adds c2 for entry->x04/archive indexing
human_pr_text: notes earlier tydisplay string-base/assert reshaping for un_8031B1FC was dropped and source string literals plus HSD_ASSERT were kept
text_corpus: bot report lists un_8031BC54 as 99.73% to 100.00%

key_files.role: Adjusted AXDriver_8038BF6C float expression grouping/order for a near-match improvement.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: tmp2 changes from left_inv_sqrt * right_sqrt to left_inv_sqrt * right_inv_sqrt and final argument expression changes to right_sqrt * left_inv_sqrt
text_corpus: bot report lists AXDriver_8038BF6C as 99.62% to 99.84%

key_files.role: Changed hsd_80393DA0 zeroing size to exclude x24 from the struct memset before assigning output buffer fields.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: memset(&hsd_804CF7E8, 0, sizeof(hsd_804CF7E8)) changed to sizeof(hsd_804CF7E8) - sizeof(hsd_804CF7E8.x24)
text_corpus: bot report lists hsd_80393DA0 as 99.78% to 99.81%

what_changed: Touched 12 files: camera.c, ftCo_Bury.c, gm_1832.c, gmregclear.c, gmstaffroll.c, mncount.c, mnitemsw.c, mnmain.c, mplib.c, tydisplay.c, axdriver.c, and particle.c.

Added or adjusted narrow stack/local-shape tactics: PAD_STACK changes in Camera_80030CFC, fn_8018325C, and mnCount_8025092C; local declaration ordering in gmregclear, gmstaffroll, mnmain, mnitemsw, tydisplay, and mplib.
Added force_active .sdata2 literals in ftCo_Bury.c for ftCo_804D8C28, ftCo_804D8C30, ftCo_804D8C38, and ftCo_804D8C3C.
Changed gm_1832 fn_801884F8 from typed result_cache field access to local int* pointer indexing at offsets 65 and 66.
Replaced expanded mpCheckFloor distance temporary chain with SQ macro expressions assigned to a shared dist2 local.
Kept TY display source string literals and HSD_ASSERT form for earlier un_8031B1FC work according to the PR body, though that earlier diff is not included in the changed hunks shown.

smart_moves.text: The PR explicitly scoped risky force_active data literals to ftCo_Bury.c and tied them to existing symbols.txt entries and the split-owned .sdata2 range instead of presenting them as general style.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: ftCo_Bury force_active .sdata2 literals correspond to existing symbols.txt entries and ftCo_Bury.c split-owned .sdata2 range
raw_diff: force_active block is confined to four constants in ftCo_Bury.c

smart_moves.text: The PR dropped earlier TY display string-base/assert reshaping and retained source string literals plus HSD_ASSERT form, aligning with conservative literal/assert cleanup standards.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: earlier tydisplay string-base/assert reshaping for un_8031B1FC was dropped
decomp_standards: no-string-literal-symbol-regression
decomp_standards: assert-report-macros

smart_moves.text: The PR used a nearby local analogy to justify an otherwise less-readable pointer-indexing tactic in gm_1832.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: pointer indexing in fn_801884F8 mirrors nearby pointer-style access in fn_8018846C
raw_diff: fn_801884F8 introduces int* ptr = (int*) &lbl_80473700

smart_moves.text: Verification evidence was recorded in the PR body and bot comment, including build success, regression gate status, exact matches, improvements, and zero reported regressions.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: git diff --check, ninja build/GALE01/main.dol OK, regression gate PR-ready
text_corpus: bot report lists +1608 code bytes, +24 data bytes, 5 new matches, 8 improvements

decomp_lessons.lesson: For split-up matching PRs, stack/local-shape edits can be acceptable when they are narrow and paired with explicit regression evidence; do not generalize PAD_STACK or declaration churn as normal style.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: stack/local-shape tweaks are kept narrow and backed by regression report
raw_diff: PAD_STACK/local declaration edits across camera.c, gm_1832.c, mncount.c, gmregclear.c, gmstaffroll.c
decomp_standards: matching-tactics-need-evidence

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a code match pressures data literals, keep data additions tightly scoped and document section ownership/symbol evidence; this PR presents ftCo_Bury .sdata2 as tied to existing symbols and split-owned range.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: ftCo_Bury force_active literals correspond to existing symbols.txt entries and split-owned .sdata2 range
raw_diff: ftCo_Bury.c force_active block
decomp_standards: literals-and-data-ownership
decomp_standards: data-sections-and-tu-splits

decomp_lessons.lesson: Macro cleanup can improve matching/readability when it expresses the same operation and is backed by score evidence; mpCheckFloor replaced manual dx/dy square temporaries with SQ expressions and improved the unmatched item.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mpCheckFloor uses SQ(px_sp54 - ax) + SQ(py_sp50 - ay)
text_corpus: bot report lists mpCheckFloor improvement from 99.68% to 99.88%
decomp_standards: canonical-control-flow-and-macros

decomp_lessons.lesson: Retaining source string literals and canonical assert macros is preferable to string-base/assert reshaping unless the PR has explicit data ownership evidence and scope.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: earlier tydisplay string-base/assert reshaping was dropped; source string literals and HSD_ASSERT form kept
decomp_standards: no-string-literal-symbol-regression
decomp_standards: assert-report-macros

naming_conventions.convention: This PR preserved mostly address-style function and data names such as fn_801884F8, lbl_80473700, mnCount_8025092C, hsd_80393DA0, and ftCo_804D8C28 rather than inventing semantic names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: address-style symbols appear throughout changed hunks
decomp_standards: conservative-naming

naming_conventions.convention: Short local role names remained in use where already clear or common, such as gobj, cobj, jobj, fp, data, tbl, root, child, cat, and id.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: local variables in mnitemsw.c, mnmain.c, tydisplay.c, ftCo_Bury.c, gmstaffroll.c

naming_conventions.convention: Temporary argument copies were named with trailing underscores in mnItemSw_8023453C: arg1_ and arg2_. The PR evidence supports this as a local codegen tactic, not a global naming standard.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mnitemsw.c adds u8 arg1_ = arg1 and u8 arg2_ = arg2

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use explicit PAD_STACK and small padding arrays to adjust stack layout when verified by regression output.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Camera_80030CFC adds u8 _PAD[12] and PAD_STACK(2)
raw_diff: fn_8018325C adds PAD_STACK(4)
raw_diff: mnCount_8025092C adds PAD_STACK(4)
text_corpus: bot report lists new matches and improvements with no regressions

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use scoped blocks around locals to affect lifetime/stack layout without changing visible control flow.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mnCount_8025092C wraps local arrays, tmp, and loop variables inside an inner block

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Declaration ordering and local splitting can shift codegen; examples include moving temp after i in gmregclear, swapping gobj/cobj in gmstaffroll, introducing top earlier in mnmain, and adding c2 in tydisplay.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gmregclear.c fn_8017F2A4 declaration order changes
raw_diff: gmstaffroll.c gm_801AC6D8_OnEnter declaration order changes
raw_diff: mnmain.c mn_8022B3A0 declaration order changes
raw_diff: tydisplay.c un_8031BC54 adds c2 and moves id

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Pointer indexing into a global can match local codegen when supported by nearby style, but this PR's evidence is local to gm_1832 fn_801884F8.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_801884F8 uses int* ptr = (int*) &lbl_80473700 and ptr[65]/ptr[66]
human_pr_text: says this mirrors nearby pointer-style access in fn_8018846C

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Expression regrouping can improve near matches: AXDriver_8038BF6C changed multiplication pairings and mpCheckFloor consolidated squared-distance calculations through SQ.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: axdriver.c AXDriver_8038BF6C expression changes
raw_diff: mplib.c mpCheckFloor SQ expression changes
text_corpus: bot report lists both functions as improved unmatched items

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: For data-section matching, force_active was used in a narrow on/reset block around known ftCo_Bury .sdata2 constants.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_Bury.c force_active on/reset block
human_pr_text: literals correspond to existing symbols.txt entries and split-owned .sdata2 range

evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Reviewer Notes

review_feedback.text: The PR notes gm_1832 pointer indexing in fn_801884F8 was chosen to match codegen and mirror nearby fn_8018846C pointer-style access.

review_feedback.text: Decomp bot reported 5 new matches, 8 improved unmatched items, +1608 matched code bytes, +24 matched data bytes, and no listed broken/fuzzy/metric regressions.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching fn_801884F8 or lbl_80473700 to see whether ptr[65]/ptr[66] was replaced with typed fields.
Search ftCo_804D8C28 ftCo_804D8C30 ftCo_804D8C38 ftCo_804D8C3C for subsequent data ownership or symbol metadata cleanup.
Search un_8031B1FC tydisplay string-base assert reshaping to find the earlier dropped approach and any follow-up review.
Search PAD_STACK changes in Camera_80030CFC mnCount_8025092C fn_8018325C for later cleanup or verifier notes.
Search mpCheckFloor SQ dist2 for whether the SQ macro form remained stable in later matching work.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR identity, state, author, timestamps, changed file list
human_pr_text: PR summary, reviewer notes, verification
raw_diff: complete diff excerpt for all 12 changed files
text_corpus: bot issue comment with exact matches and improvement table
notes: No human review comments or review approvals are present in the loaded slice; review_feedback is limited to author reviewer notes and bot output.
The PR body claims local regression gate and ninja success; intake did not rerun verification.
Some tactics, especially pointer indexing and PAD_STACK/local-shape changes, are supported as local matching choices in this PR but should not be promoted as general style.
The ftCo_Bury .sdata2 ownership claim is from the PR body; no symbols.txt or split metadata file was loaded for independent confirmation.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep stack/local-shape tactics narrow and evidence-backed
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2636 used PAD_STACK, padding locals, declaration order changes, scoped locals, and temporary argument copies as narrow matching tactics, and paired them with build/regression evidence. Treat this as evidence for narrow, verified use rather than normal coding style.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: stack/local-shape tweaks are kept narrow and backed by regression report
text_corpus: bot report lists no broken matches, no fuzzy regressions, no metric regressions
raw_diff: PAD_STACK/local-shape edits across touched files

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefer source literals/assert macros unless data ownership is explicit
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2636 explicitly dropped a TY display string-base/assert reshaping attempt and kept source string literals plus HSD_ASSERT form, reinforcing that string-symbol/data reshaping needs explicit ownership evidence and scope.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: earlier tydisplay string-base/assert reshaping for un_8031B1FC was dropped
decomp_standards: no-string-literal-symbol-regression
decomp_standards: assert-report-macros

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use local analogies when choosing unusual access shapes
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2636 justified gm_1832 fn_801884F8 pointer indexing by citing nearby pointer-style access in fn_8018846C. This is useful as a local-evidence pattern, not as blanket approval for raw pointer indexing.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: pointer indexing mirrors nearby pointer-style access in fn_8018846C
raw_diff: fn_801884F8 uses int* ptr and ptr[65]/ptr[66]

source_update_candidates.title: ftCo_Bury.c owns a split .sdata2 range used by ftCo_804D8C28/30/38/3C
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2636 states that ftCo_Bury force_active .sdata2 literals correspond to existing symbols.txt entries and the ftCo_Bury.c split-owned .sdata2 range. Curator should verify against symbols/splits before adding as a path fact.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: ftCo_Bury adds tightly scoped force_active .sdata2 literals corresponding to existing symbols.txt entries and split-owned .sdata2 range
raw_diff: adds ftCo_804D8C28, ftCo_804D8C30, ftCo_804D8C38, ftCo_804D8C3C in force_active block
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a source/path ownership fact claimed by the PR body but not independently verified in the loaded slice.

source_update_candidates.title: gm_1832 fn_801884F8 may share pointer-indexing style with nearby fn_8018846C
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2636 states fn_801884F8 uses local pointer indexing to match codegen, mirroring nearby pointer-style access in fn_8018846C. Curator should verify current gm_1832 source before recording a local-style/path fact.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: gm_1832 pointer indexing mirrors nearby pointer-style access in fn_8018846C
raw_diff: fn_801884F8 changes result_cache field access to ptr[65]/ptr[66]
source_update_candidates.reason: The local analogy is PR-supported but should be checked against source before becoming a path fact.

rejection_notes: Do not promote force_active literals as a general tactic; this PR only supports a tightly scoped ftCo_Bury case with claimed symbol/split evidence.
Do not promote pointer indexing over typed fields globally; fn_801884F8 is a local matching tactic justified by a nearby analogy, and typed-field standards still apply when types are known.
Do not treat trailing-underscore argument copies or PAD_STACK changes as naming/style standards; they are local matching tactics backed by this PR's regression evidence.
Do not claim independent validation beyond the loaded PR body and bot report; intake did not run builds, objdiff, or regression tools.
